sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.18 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$R_{p^k}$be the variety of class$2$nilpotent groups of exponent$p^k$, where$p$is a prime number and$k 2$. It is true that for every$p$and$k$there are infinitely many subquasivarieties of$R_{p^k}$each of which is generated by a finite group with derived subgroup of exponent$p^k$and does not have an independent basis of quasi-identities? { A.\,I.\,Budkin}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
